newsSinceJun6: No material company-specific news in the Jun 6-9 window. Today's +3.4% move (HKD 72.55 -> 75.00) looks flow-driven: SMIC posted the highest net southbound Stock Connect inflow among active stocks (~HK$943M) amid a broader HK chip bid. The $5.97B CSRC approval to buy in the remaining 49% of Beijing unit SMNC (547.2M new A-shares) landed May 22, pre-window, and Q2 guidance of +14-16% revenue on AI demand and price hikes was already known. YTD is flat (-0.13%) and the stock sits ~20% below its 52-week high of HKD 93.50.

sources.label: Yahoo Finance - 0981.HK quote and Jun 9 price action
SCMP - SMIC clears final hurdle for US$6B SMNC takeover (May 22, 2026)
Investing.com - SMIC quote, analyst ratings, Stock Connect flows
SCMP - SMIC action plan for new growth opportunities

verdicts.rankRationale: Hold at 52: no new fundamental evidence since Jun 6, and today's +3.4% is southbound-flow chasing, not a CPO-relevant development. With YTD flat and the SMNC consolidation (EPS-accretive, 12-inch capacity) already approved pre-window, the setup is unchanged from yesterday's hold; the page's core objection - CPO exposure is indirect and tool-constrained - still binds.
verdicts.bull: Full ownership of SMNC's 12-inch Beijing fab plus Q2 guidance of +14-16% revenue on AI demand and pricing power show China localization is monetizing; flat YTD means none of this re-rating is in the price versus peers.
verdicts.neutral: Solid domestic-foundry story but a weak CPO vehicle: silicon photonics/advanced packaging exposure is indirect, and the alpha on this page belongs to names closer to the optical engine. Flow-driven 3% days neither confirm nor break the thesis.
verdicts.bear: Export controls cap the node roadmap and tool access; if AI-localization sentiment cools, a $96B market cap foundry trading on policy optionality with structurally lower margins than TSMC de-rates quickly.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Evidence of SMIC packaging/photonics content in domestic AI accelerator ramps (Huawei ecosystem), or Q2 results beating the +14-16% guide in early August.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Tightened US/allied export controls on tools or a reversal of southbound flows that have been the marginal buyer of the HK line.
